## Input

Top-5 MCP servers that research-scout has recommended for immediate import (see DEV-86 for the full inventory). Before wiring sub-issues (DEV-87 through DEV-91) are promoted from backlog to todo, these need a security and maintainer-health review.

### Candidates to deep-dive:

1. **GitHub MCP** — [github/github-mcp-server](https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server)
2. **Supabase MCP** — [supabase-community/supabase-mcp](https://github.com/supabase-community/supabase-mcp)
3. **Playwright MCP** — [microsoft/playwright-mcp](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp)
4. **Context7 MCP** — [upstash/context7](https://github.com/upstash/context7)
5. **Sentry MCP** — [getsentry/sentry-mcp](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-mcp)

Additionally, please verify + locate GitHub URLs for three candidates flagged as "URL pending verification" in DEV-86:
- **Exa MCP** — Exa semantic search (exa.ai)
- **Resend MCP** — Transactional email
- **Brave Search MCP** — confirm whether an actively-maintained community fork exists post-archive

## Expected Output

Per each of the top-5 candidates:
- **License**: confirm OSI-approved (Apache 2, MIT, etc.)
- **Maintainer activity**: last commit date, open issues count, release cadence
- **Security posture**: any known CVEs, supply chain risk (dependency count, lockfile present), permissions requested vs what is actually needed
- **Verdict**: APPROVE / APPROVE WITH CONDITIONS / REJECT — with a one-line reason

For the 3 "URL pending" candidates: provide the confirmed GitHub URL + brief health check (stars, last commit, license).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 top candidates reviewed with approve/reject verdicts
- [ ] 3 pending URLs resolved
- [ ] Results posted as a comment on this issue

## Dependencies
- DEV-86 (inventory) — already done
- Wiring sub-issues DEV-87 through DEV-91 are blocked on this review

## Risk
github-analyst may find a security issue that changes the import decision for one of the top-5. If that happens: post a comment on DEV-61 and the relevant wiring sub-issue before rejecting.

## Definition of Done
All 5 verdicts posted. Pending URLs resolved. Wiring sub-issues can be promoted.

# Top-20 MCP Server Inventory — devosnt App Factory

**Prepared by:** research-scout · **Date:** 2026-05-26  
**Parent:** DEV-61 · **Stack baseline:** Next.js + Supabase + Vercel

---

## Methodology

Surveyed 30 candidates across official registries, Anthropic/MCP reference servers, and first-party vendor implementations. Filtered against App Factory build categories (CRM, HR, delivery, booking, SaaS, dashboards, mobile) and current stack. Eliminated redundant/secondary options. 20 servers made the cut; 10 deferred or rejected with reasons.

---

## Top-20 Inventory

| # | Server | GitHub URL | Purpose | Maintainer | Perms / Tools Exposed | Risk | Recommended Agents | Decision |
|---|--------|-----------|---------|-----------|----------------------|------|-------------------|---------|
| 1 | **GitHub MCP** | [github/github-mcp-server](https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server) | Repo management, issues, PRs, code review, branch ops | GitHub (official) | Full GitHub API (read + write); scoped to PAT or GitHub App token | **Medium** — token scope is critical; restrict to specific repos | claude-code-lead-developer, frontend-engineer, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer, mobile-engineer, qa-engineer, github-analyst | **IMPORT** |
| 2 | **Supabase MCP** | [supabase-community/supabase-mcp](https://github.com/supabase-community/supabase-mcp) | DB queries, migrations, edge functions, auth, storage — full Supabase project access | Supabase Community | 20+ tools: SQL, schema, migrations, auth admin, Storage; `read_only` flag available | **High** — management API can run migrations; use project-scoped PAT + `read_only` for non-architect agents | database-architect, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer | **IMPORT** |
| 3 | **Playwright MCP** | [microsoft/playwright-mcp](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp) | Browser automation, E2E testing, visual regression, web scraping | Microsoft (official) | Browser control, screenshots, form fill, network intercept; sandboxed browser context | **Medium** — autonomous browser execution; sandbox + network policy recommended | qa-engineer, bug-hunter | **IMPORT** |
| 4 | **Context7 MCP** | [upstash/context7](https://github.com/upstash/context7) | Injects version-specific library docs into prompts; prevents hallucinated API calls | Upstash | 2 tools: `resolve-library-id`, `get-library-docs`; read-only external API call | **Low** — read-only, no data egress; requires CONTEXT7_API_KEY | frontend-engineer, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer, mobile-engineer, claude-code-lead-developer | **IMPORT** |
| 5 | **Sentry MCP** | [getsentry/sentry-mcp](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-mcp) | Error tracking, stack traces, performance telemetry for post-launch monitoring | Sentry (official) | Read error events, releases, issues; write: assign/resolve issues | **Medium** — exposes raw stack traces + user context; restrict to read for most agents | deployment-engineer, qa-engineer, security-auditor | **IMPORT** |
| 6 | **Stripe MCP** | [stripe/ai](https://github.com/stripe/ai) | Payments, subscriptions, invoices, customers — 25 tools across 13 resource types | Stripe (official) | Full Stripe API; remote hosted at mcp.stripe.com with OAuth | **High** — financial data; wire test-mode key only; prod key requires explicit approval gate | integration-architect, backend-engineer | **IMPORT** |
| 7 | **Vercel MCP** | [vercel/vercel-mcp-overview](https://github.com/vercel/vercel-mcp-overview) | Deployment status, rollback, env var management, preview URL inspection | Vercel (official) | Deploy/manage projects, list deployments, read env vars; OAuth | **Medium** — can trigger deploys; deployment-engineer should gate prod actions | deployment-engineer, environment-manager, release-manager | **IMPORT** |
| 8 | **Figma MCP** | [figma/mcp-server-guide](https://github.com/figma/mcp-server-guide) | Design specs → code; pull design tokens, layers, annotations; write-to-canvas (beta) | Figma (official, open beta) | Read design files; write-to-canvas; hosted at mcp.figma.com | **Low** — read-only in default config; write-to-canvas gated by Figma auth | ui-designer, ux-architect, frontend-engineer | **IMPORT** |
| 9 | **Cloudflare MCP** | [cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare](https://github.com/cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare) | Workers, KV, R2, D1, DNS, security rules — Cloudflare account management | Cloudflare (official) | Broad CF account access; domain-specific sub-servers available | **High** — can modify DNS + firewall rules; scope to specific zones; deployment-engineer gate | deployment-engineer, solution-architect | **IMPORT** |
| 10 | **Memory MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) | Knowledge graph persistent memory (entities + relations) per agent | Anthropic / MCP steering (official) | Local file system read/write only; no external calls | **Low** — local only; choose storage path carefully | ceo, solution-architect, memory-curator, product-manager | **IMPORT** |
| 11 | **Sequential Thinking MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) | Dynamic multi-step reasoning; structured thought chains | Anthropic / MCP steering (official) | No external access; pure computation | **Low** — stateless, no data egress | ceo, product-manager, api-architect, solution-architect | **IMPORT** |
| 12 | **Fetch MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) | Fetch + convert web pages / docs into LLM-ready markdown | Anthropic / MCP steering (official) | External HTTP GET; URL-based; no auth | **Low** — outbound HTTP; SSRF risk if user-supplied URLs not validated | research-scout, docs-analyst, github-analyst | **IMPORT** |
| 13 | **Git MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) | Local git repo operations: read history, diff, search, commits | Anthropic / MCP steering (official) | Local filesystem git ops; read + write local repo | **Low** — local only; no remote push in default config | frontend-engineer, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer, mobile-engineer | **IMPORT** |
| 14 | **Tavily MCP** | [tavily-ai/tavily-mcp](https://github.com/tavily-ai/tavily-mcp) | Production-ready web search + extract + crawl with citations | Tavily (official) | 4 tools: search, extract, map, crawl; requires TAVILY_API_KEY | **Low** — outbound only; paid per query | research-scout, docs-analyst, github-analyst | **IMPORT** |
| 15 | **PostgreSQL MCP** | [crystaldba/postgres-mcp](https://github.com/crystaldba/postgres-mcp) | Schema analysis, query tuning, direct SQL for DB introspection | crystaldba (community, active) | Direct Postgres connection; read + write; connection string required | **High** — direct DB access; use read-only DB user for non-architect agents | database-architect, backend-engineer | **IMPORT (read-only user)** |
| 16 | **Docker Hub MCP** | [docker/hub-mcp](https://github.com/docker/hub-mcp) | Container image search, repository management, Docker Hub API | Docker (official) | Docker Hub API; image pull, push, repo management | **Medium** — can push images; scope to specific org | deployment-engineer, environment-manager | **IMPORT** |
| 17 | **Brave Search MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived) | Privacy-first web search; no tracking | Anthropic ref (archived — community forks active) | Web search API; requires BRAVE_API_KEY | **Low** — read-only; note: archived, use community fork or substitute Tavily | research-scout | **IMPORT (verify active fork — github-analyst to confirm)** |
| 18 | **Exa MCP** | URL to be verified by github-analyst | Semantic neural web + code search; ideal for prior-art research | Exa (official) | Web + code search API; requires EXA_API_KEY | **Low** — read-only search | research-scout, github-analyst | **IMPORT (URL pending verification)** |
| 19 | **Google Maps MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived) | Location, routing, distance — delivery + booking apps | Google / Anthropic ref (archived) | Maps API read; GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY required | **Low** — read-only geo data | integration-architect | **IMPORT (archived — verify maintained fork)** |
| 20 | **Resend MCP** | URL to be verified by github-analyst | Transactional email sending for SaaS + notification flows | Resend (official or community) | Email send API; RESEND_API_KEY; From address required | **Medium** — email send; restrict to verified domain; rate-limit | integration-architect, backend-engineer | **IMPORT (URL pending verification)** |

---

## Rejected / Deferred (10)

| Server | Reason |
|--------|--------|
| AWS MCP (awslabs/mcp) | Stack is Vercel + Supabase; AWS is secondary. Revisit if multi-cloud. |
| MongoDB MCP | Stack uses PostgreSQL exclusively. Redundant. |
| Linear MCP | Workspace uses Multica, not Linear. |
| Discord MCP | Not in current stack; Slack preferred if comms MCP ever needed. |
| Slack MCP | Nice-to-have for team comms automation, but not on critical path now. Defer to Q3. |
| Notion MCP | Not in stack. If knowledge base need emerges, revisit. |
| Terraform MCP (HashiCorp) | IaC needs currently covered by Vercel + Cloudflare native tools. Defer. |
| dbt MCP | Analytics semantic layer; relevant only post-scale, not in build phase. |
| Filesystem MCP | Redundant — Claude Code runtime already provides local file ops natively. |
| Vantage MCP (cloud cost) | Useful post-launch for cost visibility. Defer until first production apps are live. |

---

## Top-5 Import Priority + Wiring Plan

| Priority | Server | Primary Agents to Wire | Risk Mitigation | Wiring Steps |
|----------|--------|----------------------|-----------------|-------------|
| **P1** | GitHub MCP | claude-code-lead-developer, frontend-engineer, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer, mobile-engineer, qa-engineer, github-analyst | Scope PAT to workspace repos only; read-only for QA agents | `multica agent update <id> --mcp-config ...` with token scoped per agent |
| **P2** | Supabase MCP | database-architect, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer | `project_ref` scoped per app; `read_only: true` for engineer agents; management-level only for database-architect | Set per-agent `mcp_config` with separate tokens per role |
| **P3** | Playwright MCP | qa-engineer, bug-hunter | Run in sandboxed browser; no credential storage in browser session | Add to qa-engineer + bug-hunter `mcp_config`; network policy: block internal endpoints |
| **P4** | Context7 MCP | claude-code-lead-developer, frontend-engineer, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer, mobile-engineer | Read-only; no sensitive data; API key from Upstash | Wire `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` via environment-manager; add to all code agent `mcp_config` |
| **P5** | Sentry MCP | deployment-engineer, qa-engineer, security-auditor | Read-only token for qa + security; write token only for deployment-engineer | Provision Sentry auth token per agent role |

---

## Input
Pattern imported from benchmark sweep: Bolt.new Figma import (drop Figma designs into chat → working app with visual reference).
Source brief: [DEV-76](mention://issue/ee26fb58-8f56-41fc-9543-ca2f5c230f85) (Bolt.new)

## Skill to Build: `figma-to-ux-spec`

### Purpose
Accept a Figma file URL or exported JSON/ZIP in the UX Planning stage. Extract screens, components, design tokens, and interaction annotations. Output a structured screen list + per-screen state spec that the UX Architect currently writes manually — reducing UX Planning time for design-led projects.

### Trigger
- A product brief or PRD includes a Figma file URL.
- UX Architect or product-manager explicitly calls the skill on a UX Planning issue.

### Required Input
- Figma file URL (public share link) or exported Figma JSON
- Product brief / PRD (for context mapping)

### Steps
1. Fetch Figma file via Figma REST API (or parse exported JSON).
2. Identify top-level frames as screens. Extract:
   - Screen name
   - Components present (buttons, forms, tables, navigation)
   - Design tokens (color, typography, spacing)
   - Annotations / prototype interactions
3. Map screens to user roles from the PRD permission matrix.
4. Generate per-screen spec: screen name, route, role visibility, components, states (empty/loading/error), interactions.
5. Output as structured markdown (compatible with UX Architect's screen list format).
6. Post output as comment on the UX Planning issue + attach as file.

### Output
- Sitemap derived from Figma frames
- Screen list with role visibility and state matrix
- Design token extract (colors, fonts, spacing)
- Any gaps identified (screens in PRD missing from Figma, or vice versa)

### Quality Checklist
- [ ] All top-level Figma frames mapped to screens
- [ ] Role visibility sourced from PRD permission matrix, not inferred
- [ ] Empty/loading/error states explicitly noted (or flagged as missing in Figma)
- [ ] Design token extract complete
- [ ] Gaps between PRD and Figma listed

### Failure Handling
If Figma URL is inaccessible (private/expired): prompt user for exported JSON or Figma export file. Do not skip — missing design is a blocker.

### Improvement Rule
After 3 uses, compare spec completeness (manual UX Architect review score) to baseline. If < 80% match, improve frame-to-screen heuristics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill spec written and filed in `08 - Skill Library`
- [ ] skill-builder implements skill
- [ ] Test: run against a real Figma public file and verify output matches manual UX spec format

## Dependencies
- [DEV-62](mention://issue/c289d731-eaf9-4747-814f-c122f1ed08cd) benchmark rollup (this parent)
- Figma API access (public share links should work without auth; private files need FIGMA_TOKEN env var)

## Risk
Low-Medium. Read-only Figma access. Risk: Figma API schema changes. Mitigation: pin Figma API version.

## Definition of Done
Skill filed in 08 - Skill Library. Tested with a real Figma file. No secrets committed.

## Input
Pattern imported from benchmark sweep: Replit Agent 3/4 autonomous test-fix loop (3× faster, 10× cheaper than Computer Use per Replit's benchmarks).
Source brief: [DEV-78](mention://issue/74a6652c-9293-45ca-b6f6-9ff0e2459a58) (Replit Agent)

## Skill to Build: `autonomous-test-fix-loop`

### Purpose
After a build agent ships a feature and marks it `in_review`, automatically run the QA test plan against the live preview URL, detect failures, and create a self-healing fix sub-issue assigned back to the build agent. Human only reviews when the loop converges or after a configurable max-retry limit.

### Trigger
- A build sub-issue transitions to `in_review`.
- A QA engineer has attached a test plan to the issue.

### Required Input
- Issue ID of the feature being tested
- Preview URL (from issue metadata `deploy_url` or comment)
- QA test plan (from issue description or QA sub-issue)

### Steps
1. Read the QA test plan from the issue.
2. Execute each test case against the preview URL (headless browser / Playwright).
3. If all pass: comment "All tests passed. Ready for QA review." — done.
4. If any fail: create a fix sub-issue (`--status todo`, assigned to original build agent) with the failure log.
5. On fix sub-issue `in_review`: re-run the loop from step 2.
6. After 3 failed retry cycles: escalate to qa-engineer and ceo with full failure log.

### Output
- Pass/fail result comment on the feature issue.
- Fix sub-issue (if failures found).
- Escalation comment after 3 retry failures.

### Quality Checklist
- [ ] Test runner executes all test cases from the QA plan
- [ ] Failure log captures exact assertion + screenshot
- [ ] Fix sub-issue created with correct parent link and build agent assignee
- [ ] Max-retry cap enforced (3 cycles)
- [ ] Escalation posted after cap reached

### Failure Handling
If preview URL is unreachable: post `blocked` comment with reason. Do not create fix sub-issue.

### Improvement Rule
After 5 uses, measure mean cycles-to-green. If > 2, audit QA test plan quality with qa-engineer.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill spec written and filed in `08 - Skill Library`
- [ ] skill-builder implements skill
- [ ] Integration test: one build issue with a known failing test → fix loop fires and converges

## Dependencies
- [DEV-62](mention://issue/c289d731-eaf9-4747-814f-c122f1ed08cd) benchmark rollup (this parent)

## Risk
Medium. Creates sub-issues and transitions statuses autonomously — test the retry cap carefully to avoid loops.

## Definition of Done
Skill filed in 08 - Skill Library. Tested. No secrets.
issues.title: SKILL: autonomous-test-fix-loop — browser-test + self-healing fix cycle after build

issues.description:
## Input
Pattern imported from benchmark sweep: Cursor "Build in Parallel" + Replit Agent 4 parallel task execution.
Source briefs: [DEV-77](mention://issue/e5cf1d27-3c5b-458a-967b-95b4e3f6b017) (Cursor), [DEV-78](mention://issue/74a6652c-9293-45ca-b6f6-9ff0e2459a58) (Replit Agent)

## Skill to Build: `parallel-issue-dispatcher`

### Purpose
Auto-identify independent sub-issues within a parent and batch-promote all of them to `--status todo` simultaneously. Today, the sprint-commander promotes sub-issues one-by-one; this skill reads the dependency graph and launches all non-blocked work in parallel.

### Trigger
- Any parent issue with 3+ sub-issues in `backlog` status where some are independent.
- Explicitly called by sprint-commander or ceo after a parent issue's build plan is finalized.

### Required Input
- Parent issue ID
- List of sub-issues with their declared `Dependencies` field values

### Steps
1. Read all sub-issues of parent. Parse each issue's `Dependencies` field.
2. Build a dependency DAG: which sub-issues have no upstream dependencies (or all upstream are `done`/`in_review`)?
3. Promote all dependency-free sub-issues to `--status todo` in a single batch.
4. Comment on parent: "Dispatched N sub-issues in parallel: [list]. Remaining M are backlog pending: [list]."
5. When any sub-issue reaches `done`, re-run the DAG and promote newly unblocked issues.

### Output
- All independent sub-issues promoted to `todo` in one pass.
- Parent comment with dispatch map.

### Quality Checklist
- [ ] DAG correctly identifies all independent vs. blocked issues
- [ ] No issue promoted if any upstream dependency is not `done` or `in_review`
- [ ] Parent comment posted with full dispatch map
- [ ] Re-run logic triggers on sub-issue completion

### Failure Handling
If a sub-issue has no `Dependencies` field, treat it as independent (safe default).

### Improvement Rule
After 3 uses, measure actual parallelization rate (independent/total). If < 50%, audit issue creation practices.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill spec written and filed in `08 - Skill Library`
- [ ] skill-builder implements skill with all required fields
- [ ] Tested against a real parent issue with mixed independent + blocked sub-issues

## Dependencies
- [DEV-62](mention://issue/c289d731-eaf9-4747-814f-c122f1ed08cd) benchmark rollup (this parent)

## Risk
Low. Read-only analysis + status promotion — fully reversible.

## Definition of Done
Skill filed in 08 - Skill Library. Tested. No secrets.

## Input
Phase 6 benchmark sweep — Atoms.dev vendor brief.

## Vendor: Atoms.dev
**Date**: 2026-05-26
**Sources**: https://atoms.dev | https://www.toolworthy.ai/tool/atoms-dev | https://karozieminski.substack.com/p/atoms-vs-lovable-vs-replit-ai-builder-2026

---

## Capabilities Summary
Multi-agent AI app builder. Runs a team of seven specialized AI agents (roles: PM, Architect, Engineer, SEO, Data Scientist, Team Lead, Researcher) that collaborate to plan, build, test, and launch apps.

**Key features (2026):**
- Multi-agent team: 7 roles (Iris/research, Emma/PM, Bob/architect, Alex/engineer, Mike/lead, Sarah/SEO, David/data) working collaboratively
- Race Mode (Max plans): runs multiple AI iterations simultaneously for up to 3x build accuracy on complex apps
- Full-stack: user auth, database, Stripe payments, custom domain, SSL, SEO, analytics out of the box
- No-code / natural language interface (vibe coding focus)
- 1M+ builders in ecosystem, ranked #2 Product Hunt Feb 3 2026
- Credit-based pricing: Free (25 credits/cycle) → paid tiers up to 10,000 credits/month

## What Atoms.dev beats devosnt at
- Role-specialization within a single UX: seven distinct AI roles visible and interacting in one interface — users see the "team" at work
- Race Mode: parallel model runs for accuracy on complex problems — no equivalent in our pipeline
- SEO agent built-in: every app gets SEO optimization without a separate step
- Consumer accessibility: non-technical vibe coders are the primary audience

## What devosnt beats Atoms.dev at
- Real squad ownership: our squads have accountability (issue owner, DoD, AC) — Atoms' "agents" are a UX metaphor, not persistent workers
- Security enforcement: mandatory RLS, auth, security review gate — Atoms has no equivalent gate
- Arabic/RTL + MENA market defaults
- Institutional Skill Library: knowledge compounds across builds
- Enterprise business systems complexity

## Key patterns to evaluate for import
1. **Role-labeled agent UI** — surfacing agent specialization (architect, PM, engineer) in the issue workflow so users can see who is "on" each task (maps to our squad/assignee system)
2. **Race Mode / multi-model accuracy** — run the same build task on 2-3 agents and pick the best output for critical or complex features
3. **SEO as a default build output** — wire SEO audit as a mandatory checklist item on every front-end ship, not a separate sprint

## Open-source surface
Atoms.dev is proprietary. Check GitHub for community projects / templates in the Atoms ecosystem.

[@docs-analyst](mention://agent/8ba15742-739b-4b44-b042-b56c5b7ff3eb) — please extract the latest Atoms.dev changelog/blog and verify 2026 feature list above.

[@github-analyst](mention://agent/ce249709-eb0c-4a45-ad59-007e27564dc0) — please search GitHub for any open-source Atoms.dev community templates or ecosystem repos worth surveying.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Changelog verified by docs-analyst
- [ ] GitHub ecosystem surveyed by github-analyst
- [ ] Brief finalized

## Definition of Done
Brief complete. Research cited. No secrets.

## Input
Phase 6 benchmark sweep — Antigravity 2.0 vendor brief.

## Vendor: Antigravity 2.0 (Google)
**Date**: 2026-05-26
**Sources**: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/19/google-launches-antigravity-2-0-with-an-updated-desktop-app-and-cli-tool-at-io-2026/ | https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/developers-tools/google-io-2026-developer-highlights/ | https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/05/19/google-launches-antigravity-2-0-at-i-o-2026-a-standalone-agent-first-platform-with-cli-sdk-managed-execution-and-enterprise-support/

---

## Capabilities Summary
Google's agent-first developer platform. Launched at Google I/O 2026 (May 19). Standalone desktop app + CLI + SDK + Managed Agents (Gemini API) + Enterprise platform.

**Key features (Antigravity 2.0, launched May 19 2026):**
- Desktop app: orchestrate multiple agents simultaneously, design custom subagent workflows, schedule background tasks
- Native voice command support
- Antigravity CLI: lightweight, high-velocity — create and run agents without GUI
- Antigravity SDK: build custom agents based on Google's coding tool
- Managed Agents in the Gemini API: hosted agent execution
- Enterprise: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (enterprise-grade compliance, SSO, audit)
- Deep Google ecosystem: Firebase, Android, AI Studio, Google Cloud integrations
- Powered by Gemini 3.5 Flash (co-developed using Antigravity)
- AI Ultra plan: $100/mo for 5x higher AI limits vs. Pro

## What Antigravity 2.0 beats devosnt at
- Google ecosystem integration: Firebase, Android, AI Studio, Google Cloud native — unmatched for Google-stack apps
- Enterprise platform: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform provides compliance, SSO, audit trails at scale
- Voice-first interface: native voice commands across all features
- SDK for custom agent creation: customers can extend the platform with their own agents

## User request

You are the Factory Kernel CEO of this Multica workspace.

Your mission is to turn this workspace into a world-class autonomous AI App Factory that is faster, smarter, and more complete than Lovable, Antigravity 2.0, Atoms.dev, Bolt, Replit Agent, Cursor, and normal Claude Code workflows.

You are not a chatbot. You are the operating brain of an elite AI software company.

MAIN OBJECTIVE:
When I give you any idea, you must turn it into a complete product with maximum speed and quality:
- research
- product strategy
- PRD
- UX/UI
- database
- APIs
- frontend
- backend
- auth
- integrations
- QA
- security
- deployment
- documentation
- SEO/growth
- reusable skills
- continuous improvement

OPERATING MODEL:
Use Multica as the control plane for projects, issues, squads, agents, skills, tracking, and execution.
Use Hermes as the long-running runtime for memory, planning, learning, self-improvement, and skill refinement.
Use Claude Code as the deep coding engine for repo analysis, coding, debugging, testing, refactoring, hooks, subagents, MCP, and plugins.
Use GitHub research to discover useful skills, templates, MCP tools, app-builder patterns, and open-source workflows.
Use Rainbow-style workflow:
regulate → assess-context → specify → design → taskify → implement → validate → improve.

IMMEDIATE MODE:
Enter FAST FACTORY MODE now.

PHASE 1 — FULL WORKSPACE AUDIT
Audit the entire workspace:
- projects
- issues
- squads
- agents
- skills
- runtimes
- autopilots
- templates
- context
- blocked work
- idle work
- weak outputs
- duplicated agents
- duplicated skills
- missing QA/security/deployment systems

Return:
- what works
- what is incomplete
- what is weak
- what is duplicated
- what is missing
- what is risky
- what can run in parallel immediately

PHASE 2 — COMMAND CENTER
Create a live Command Center.

Track every project:
- goal
- phase
- priority
- active issues
- squad
- agents
- status
- blockers
- risks
- next action
- expected output
- quality gate
- approval needed or not

Track every issue:
- title
- owner
- squad
- priority
- input
- output
- dependencies
- acceptance criteria
- definition of done
- current status
- next action

No task can stay vague, idle, unassigned, blocked, or completed without review.

PHASE 3 — PARALLEL EXECUTION ENGINE
For every new app/system idea, split work into these parallel tracks:

1. Product Track — Owner: Product Squad
Output: product brief, market/use-case validation, MVP scope, production scope, PRD, user stories, acceptance criteria, role matrix.

2. UX/UI Track — Owner: UX Squad
Output: sitemap, screen list, user flows, dashboard layout, mobile layout, empty states, loading states, error states, RTL/Arabic rules when needed.

3. Architecture Track — Owner: Architecture Squad
Output: recommended stack, database schema, ERD, API contracts, auth model, permissions, integrations, security model.

4. Build Track — Owner: Build Squad
Output: frontend, backend, database setup, auth, APIs, integrations, tests, code review.

5. QA/Security Track — Owner: QA Security Squad
Output: test plan, test cases, bug reports, permission review, security review, performance review.

6. DevOps/Docs Track — Owner: Launch Squad
Output: environment checklist, deployment plan, rollback plan, release notes, admin guide, user guide, handover checklist.

7. Growth Track — Owner: Growth Squad
Output: landing page plan, SEO plan, onboarding flow, analytics events, conversion checklist, launch campaign plan.

8. Skill Factory Track — Owner: Skill Factory Squad
Output: reusable skills, improved prompts, improved issue templates, improved agent instructions, post-build learning report.

Execution rules:
- Start Product, UX, and Architecture together.
- Start QA planning before coding is complete.
- Start DevOps planning before final QA.
- Start Growth planning before launch.
- Start Build once architecture is clear enough.
- Start Skill Factory as soon as a workflow repeats.
- Never wait if another safe parallel track can move.

PHASE 4 — GITHUB RESEARCH ENGINE
Continuously research and evaluate:
- Multica latest docs/changelog/issues
- Hermes Agent latest docs/releases
- Claude Code skills/hooks/subagents/MCP/plugins
- Anthropic official skills
- Supabase agent skills
- awesome-agent-skills
- awesome-claude-code-toolkit
- awesome-claude-code-subagents
- hanoi-rainbow
- open-source AI app builders
- open-source agent orchestrators
- Next.js/Supabase/TypeScript templates
- MCP servers useful for app building
- Lovable, Antigravity, Atoms.dev, Bolt, Replit, Cursor as benchmarks

For every source:
- summarize what it does
- identify useful patterns
- identify useful skills/templates
- evaluate compatibility
- identify risks
- decide: import, adapt, recreate, study only, or reject

Do not blindly install anything. Do not trust community skills until reviewed.

PHASE 5 — SKILL FACTORY
Create and improve reusable skills.

Core skills to create/improve now:
- idea-to-product-brief
- product-brief-to-prd
- prd-to-user-stories
- user-stories-to-issues
- ux-screen-generator
- database-schema-designer
- api-contract-designer
- auth-permission-matrix
- nextjs-supabase-builder
- crm-system-builder
- hr-system-builder
- delivery-system-builder
- dashboard-builder
- admin-panel-builder
- qa-test-plan
- security-review
- deployment-checklist
- growth-launch-plan
- post-build-learning-report
- agent-prompt-improver
- workspace-upgrade-loop

Every skill must include: name, purpose, trigger, inputs, steps, tools, output format, quality checklist, failure handling, security notes, improvement rule.

After every task ask:
- can this become a reusable skill?
- can this existing skill be improved?
- can this workflow become a template?
- did any agent need better instructions?
- can the next run be faster?

PHASE 6 — BENCHMARK ENGINE
Benchmark this workspace against: Lovable, Antigravity 2.0, Atoms.dev, Bolt, Replit Agent, Cursor, Claude Code-only workflows.

For each benchmark, extract:
- what they do better
- what we can copy as a workflow
- what we can beat
- what skill/template/agent we need
- what should be added to the App Factory

Goal: Do not copy them blindly. Build a stronger system using Multica squads, Hermes memory, Claude Code execution, GitHub skills, QA gates, Growth engine, and self-improvement.

PHASE 7 — QUALITY GATES
No app/system is complete unless it has: clear product goal, PRD, user stories, screen list, database schema, API plan, auth/roles, permissions, responsive UI, validation, loading states, empty states, error states, QA checklist, security review, performance review, deployment plan, rollback plan, documentation, SEO/growth plan, reusable skill extraction.

PHASE 8 — SAFETY RULES
Never: expose secrets; edit credentials without approval; delete data without approval; deploy to production without approval; install unknown tools without evaluation; run destructive commands without approval; import external skills without security review; mark work complete without quality gates; create duplicate agents or skills without reason.

PHASE 9 — RECURRING AUTOPILOTS
Create or propose recurring operations:

Daily: Command Center review, blocked issue review, idle agent review, completed output review, top priority execution plan.
Weekly: GitHub research update, benchmark report, skill library cleanup, agent performance review, workspace upgrade plan, security review.
Monthly: architecture review, tool stack review, App Factory playbook update, roadmap refresh.

If you cannot create autopilots directly, give exact manual setup steps.

START NOW:
1. Re-audit the workspace.
2. Build the Command Center.
3. Identify critical gaps.
4. Create the first 20 issues.
5. Assign issues to squads and agents.
6. Launch safe parallel work.
7. Create or improve the first 20 skills.
8. Start GitHub research tasks.
9. Start benchmark research.
10. Create the 24-hour execution plan.
11. Create the 7-day upgrade plan.
12. Tell me exactly what needs my approval.

FINAL OUTPUT FORMAT:
Return only:
1. Workspace Status
2. Critical Gaps
3. Command Center
4. Parallel Execution Plan
5. First 20 Issues To Launch
6. First 20 Skills To Create or Improve
7. GitHub Research Tasks
8. Benchmark Plan
9. Quality Gates
10. Autopilots To Create
11. 24-Hour Execution Plan
12. 7-Day Upgrade Plan
13. What Requires User Approval
14. Exact Next Action

STYLE:
Be fast. Be specific. Be practical. No long theory. No fake completion. No vague advice. Always assign owners. Always create next actions. Always improve the machine.

## Goal
Take the existing `attendance-app/` Expo source (from [DEV-33](mention://issue/f76d10de-667b-48f8-ae1b-d3baa473fee5)) and produce a signed, sideloadable `attendance-demo-v0.1.0.apk` wired to the demo Supabase URL deployment-engineer publishes on parent [DEV-42](mention://issue/2ad5f42d-1c32-48ad-aff8-5b9c071c5db5).

## Coordination
- deployment-engineer (on [DEV-42](mention://issue/2ad5f42d-1c32-48ad-aff8-5b9c071c5db5)) will post `demo_backend_url` as a metadata key + comment as soon as it's live. **Poll that issue every 5 min** until the URL appears, then proceed.
- Do NOT block on Firebase. Set `FCM_ENABLED=false` in `.env.production` — already supported by the FCM registration module (graceful no-op).
- Do NOT block on EAS / Expo cloud. Local build only.

## Build steps
1. `git clone` or restore the attendance-app worktree (check the project resource on App Hub `09e6cfd3-d45e-4225-a494-2e3400b6983c`). If unrecoverable, regenerate the scaffold from [DEV-33](mention://issue/f76d10de-667b-48f8-ae1b-d3baa473fee5) description — the AC list is the spec.
2. Install Android SDK 34 + build-tools 34.0.0 + platform-tools via `sdkmanager` (one-off). Java 17 is on the host already.
3. `expo prebuild --platform android --clean`
4. Edit `android/app/build.gradle` to ensure `versionCode 1`, `versionName "0.1.0-demo"`, applicationId `com.newtechkw.attendance.demo` (suffix `.demo` to allow side-by-side with future prod).
5. Generate debug keystore (or use the one shipped by `expo prebuild`): `keytool -genkey -v -keystore android/app/debug.keystore -alias androiddebugkey -storepass android -keypass android -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -validity 10000 -dname "CN=Demo,OU=NewTech,O=NewTech,L=Kuwait,ST=KW,C=KW"`
6. `cd android && ./gradlew assembleRelease` (release variant signed with debug keystore — fine for sideload).
7. Output: `android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk` → rename to `attendance-demo-v0.1.0.apk`.
8. Attach the APK to **[DEV-42](mention://issue/2ad5f42d-1c32-48ad-aff8-5b9c071c5db5)** via `multica issue comment add ... --attachment ./attendance-demo-v0.1.0.apk`.
9. Smoke test: install on Android emulator (API 34, x86_64) via `adb install -r`, launch, confirm login screen renders in RTL Arabic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] APK file ≤ 60 MB
- [ ] `adb install` succeeds on a fresh emulator
- [ ] App launches without crash; login screen visible in Arabic RTL
- [ ] Attempting login with seeded `super_admin@diwan.demo / Demo2026!` returns a session token from the demo backend

## Self-heal
- Two `gradle assembleRelease` failures with the same error → swap to `npx eas-cli build --platform android --profile preview --local` (still local, no cloud) before escalating.
- If Android SDK install fails on this runtime, escalate to [@ceo](mention://agent/d9f942b9-e245-4e28-99eb-8f12fbebc2c3) with the exact error — we'll swap to a runtime that has it pre-installed.

## DoD
APK attached to [DEV-42](mention://issue/2ad5f42d-1c32-48ad-aff8-5b9c071c5db5) + smoke-test screenshot + status flipped to `in_review`. CEO picks it up from there and pushes to user.

# Skill — `app-hub-bootstrap`

**Trigger:** A new App Request lands in `01 - App Requests` AND classifier outputs `vertical: <any>`. Run **before** `app-idea-to-product-brief`.

**Required input:** App slug (kebab-case), display name (English + Arabic), one-line purpose.

## Steps

1. **Create the App Hub project**
   ```bash
   multica project create \
     --title "APP — <Display Name>" \
     --icon "<emoji>" \
     --description "App Hub for <Display Name>. All execution lives here (per ADR-002)." \
     --lead ceo \
     --output json
   ```
   Capture the returned `id` as `<APP_PROJECT_ID>`.

2. **Provision the GitHub repo** (escalates to admin if no PAT in runtime env)
   - `gh repo create newtechkw/<slug> --template newtechkw/app-template --private --description "<one-liner>"`
   - Seed branches: `main` (default, protected) + `integrate` (continuous-integration target).
   - If `GH_TOKEN` is missing → open an escalation sub-issue on the parent App Request mentioning workspace-operations-manager with the exact `gh` command, **do not proceed without the repo**.

3. **Attach repo to hub**
   ```bash
   multica project resource add <APP_PROJECT_ID> \
     --type github_repo \
     --url https://github.com/newtechkw/<slug> \
     --default-branch-hint main
   ```

4. **Initialize the codegraph baseline**
   ```bash
   multica repo checkout https://github.com/newtechkw/<slug> --ref main
   cd <slug>
   codegraph init && codegraph index
   npx understand-anything init && npx understand-anything scan
   git add .codegraph .understand && git commit -m "chore: codegraph + understand baseline" && git push
   ```

5. **Pin the metadata contract on the parent App Request issue**
   - `app_project_id=<APP_PROJECT_ID>`
   - `app_repo_url=https://github.com/newtechkw/<slug>`
   - `lifecycle_phase=intake`
   - `integration_branch=integrate`
   - `local_run_command=<inferred from template>` (e.g. `pnpm dev`, `expo start`)
   - `deploy_gate=blocked: pre-build`
   - `codegraph_status=fresh@<ISO>` (set by the init in step 4)

6. **Hand off to Product UX Squad** by reassigning the parent issue.
   The Product UX Squad runs `app-idea-to-product-brief` and writes the brief **inside the App Hub project**, not in `02 - Product Specs`.

## Output

- One Multica project (`APP — <name>`) ready to accept sub-issues.
- One GitHub repo with codegraph + understand baseline.
- Parent App Request issue carries the full metadata contract.
- Comment on the parent: "App Hub bootstrap complete. Project: [link]. Repo: <url>. Handing to Product UX."

## Quality checklist

- [ ] Project exists with correct title + icon + lead.
- [ ] Repo exists, both branches present, branch protection on `main`.
- [ ] `.codegraph/codegraph.db` and `.understand/graph.json` exist on `main`.
- [ ] Parent issue has all 7 metadata keys pinned.
- [ ] Repo attached to project (visible in `multica project resource list <APP_PROJECT_ID>`).

## Failure handling

- Missing GH PAT → escalation issue + halt. Do not silently skip the repo step.
- Repo create failed (name taken) → append `-v2` and retry; pin the actual name in metadata.
- codegraph install missing on runtime → open issue against workspace-operations-manager to install on the runtime image.

## Improvement rule

If the same app vertical (e.g. CRM, HR, delivery) bootstraps ≥3 times with the same template, promote that template variant via `template-builder` (e.g. `app-template-crm`, `app-template-hr`).

issues.description:
# ADR-002 — App Hub Model (per-app project + per-app repo + codegraph brain + local-first lifecycle)

**Status:** Adopted 2026-05-25.
**Supersedes:** Default routing where every Build child lands in `05 - Parallel Build`.
**Driver:** [DEV-36](mention://issue/e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) — workspace owner asked for a coherent per-project structure, local-first integration, single integrated deploy at the end, and a code-graph layer in the style of [Understand-Anything](https://github.com/Lum1104/Understand-Anything) and [codegraph](https://github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph).

---

## 1. The change in one line
**Every app/system gets its own Multica project + its own GitHub repo + its own codegraph index. Phase projects (00–09) become the workspace's *library*, not the place where work lives.**

---

## 2. New project taxonomy

| Layer | Purpose | Examples |
|-------|---------|----------|
| **Library projects** (`00 - Factory Brain` … `09 - Research Intelligence`) | Standards, ADRs, skills, templates, research. Long-lived. Cross-app. | ADRs, the Skill Library, intake forms. |
| **App Hub projects** (`APP — <name>`) | One per app/system. **All execution issues for that app live here** — PRD, schema, build, QA, deploy, docs. Closes when the app is shipped. | `APP — Android Attendance`, `APP — HR Dashboard`, … |

The current scattered routing (PRD in `02`, schema in `04`, build children in `05`, QA in `06`, deploy in `07`) is replaced with: **every artifact for app X lives in `APP — X` and carries a `phase=<intake|spec|design|arch|build|integrate|qa|deploy|done>` label.** Cross-app skill/standard updates still live in the library projects.

## 3. Per-app GitHub repo

- Convention: `newtechkw/<app-slug>` (e.g. `newtechkw/attendance-app`).
- Created **once** at the Architecture Gate by `deployment-engineer` (escalation if no PAT).
- Attached to the app hub via `multica project resource add <hub-project-id> --type github_repo --url <repo-url>`.
- Default branch `main`. Two protected branches: `main` (deploy target) and `integrate` (continuous integration target).
- Pinned on the app's parent issue as `app_repo_url`.

## 4. Local-first lifecycle (the hard rule)

Build engineers run **everything locally** until the parent is in `in_review` with QA + Security green. No `vercel deploy`, no `eas build --profile production`, no `npm publish`, no `supabase db push --linked`. Preview deploys (`vercel --prod=false`, `eas build --profile preview`, local Supabase via Docker) are allowed and encouraged for integration testing.

Phases (mirrored in the parent issue metadata `lifecycle_phase`):

```
intake → spec → design → arch → build → integrate → qa → deploy → done
```

- `build` ends only when every build sub-issue is `done` AND the codegraph reports no orphan endpoints / missing handlers.
- `integrate` = one combined PR (or one weekly integration PR) merging every build stream into `integrate` branch + green local E2E run.
- `qa` = QA Security Squad veto gate on the `integrate` branch, not on a deployed environment.
- `deploy` = **single integrated deploy**, owned by DevOps Launch Squad, executes only after QA + Security sign-off on `integrate`.

## 5. Codegraph layer (the shared brain)

Inspired by [codegraph](https://github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph) (SQLite + tree-sitter + MCP) and [Understand-Anything](https://github.com/Lum1104/Understand-Anything) (multi-agent indexer + JSON knowledge graph).

Per repo:
- `.codegraph/codegraph.db` (SQLite, tree-sitter symbol/edge graph, FTS5).
- `.understand/graph.json` (architectural overview + business-domain map).
- Refreshed by the `codegraph-indexer` agent on every PR merge to `integrate` (and on demand).
- Exposed to other agents via MCP (`codegraph_search`, `codegraph_trace`, `codegraph_callers`, `codegraph_impact`) — wires into Build + QA agents so they reason on indexed symbols instead of grepping. Net effect from the upstream project: ~35% fewer tokens, ~71% fewer tool calls.

Owner: **Knowledge Graph Squad** (new — see §7).

## 6. Per-app metadata contract

Pinned on the **app's parent issue** (e.g. DEV-25):

| Key | Example | Why |
|-----|---------|-----|
| `app_project_id` | `<hub-uuid>` | Pointer to the per-app Multica project. |
| `app_repo_url` | `https://github.com/newtechkw/attendance-app` | Single source of truth for the codebase. |
| `lifecycle_phase` | `build` | Drives Factory Pulse routing. |
| `local_run_command` | `pnpm dev` or `expo start` | What a fresh agent runs to verify locally. |
| `integration_branch` | `integrate` | QA target before deploy. |
| `codegraph_status` | `fresh@2026-05-25T18:00Z` | Last indexer run timestamp. |
| `deploy_gate` | `blocked: qa-pending` / `green` | Hard gate read by `deployment-engineer`. |
| `waiting_on` | (existing) | External blockers (credentials, paid resources). |

## 7. New squad: Knowledge Graph Squad

Members:
- **codegraph-indexer** (leader) — runs codegraph + understand-anything pipelines on PR merge, refreshes `.codegraph/codegraph.db` and `.understand/graph.json`, posts a diff-impact summary on the merge issue.
- **codebase-cartographer** — owns the human-readable architecture overview (`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`), the guided tours, the symbol naming conventions, and the cross-repo symbol vocabulary.
- **impact-analyst** — on every Build sub-issue, runs `codegraph_impact` against the proposed diff scope and posts the affected-symbols list so reviewers know the blast radius.

Trigger: PR opened or merged on any `APP — *` repo. Skill: `codegraph-index-refresh` (to be authored).

## 8. New skill: `app-hub-bootstrap`

Steps:
1. Create `APP — <name>` Multica project (icon, description, lead = ceo).
2. Provision GitHub repo `newtechkw/<slug>` from template (template repo to be set up: includes Next.js + Supabase + Expo skeleton + codegraph install script + Understand-Anything CLI). **Requires GH PAT.**
3. Attach repo to project via `multica project resource add`.
4. Seed `main` and `integrate` branches.
5. Run `codegraph init` + `understand-anything init` on the empty repo so future merges have a baseline.
6. Pin the metadata contract on the parent app issue.
7. Hand to Product UX Squad for PRD.

## 9. Factory Pulse v3 (incremental, not destructive)

Add to existing autopilot **without changing current exclusions**:
- Read every issue's `app_project_id` metadata; route dispatch by `lifecycle_phase` first, falling back to current rules.
- New rule: when parent `lifecycle_phase=build` and every build child is `done`, flip to `integrate`, comment "Build complete — opening integration PR" and reassign the parent to Knowledge Graph Squad to run impact analysis + open the integration PR.
- New rule: never reassign the parent to DevOps Launch Squad until both `deploy_gate=green` AND `lifecycle_phase=qa→deploy`.
- DEV-36 + member-conversation exclusions remain.

## 10. What requires admin (credentials)

These are the only blockers — every other piece of this ADR is executable today:

1. **GitHub PAT** with `repo:create` scope on `newtechkw` org (so `app-hub-bootstrap` can create repos unattended).
2. **Codegraph + Understand-Anything install on the runtime host** — both are MIT-licensed npm/Python packages; the install is reversible but it modifies the runtime image.
3. Re-confirmation that **`newtechkw` is the canonical GitHub org**, not a personal account.

## 11. Migration plan for the apps already in flight

- **DEV-25 (Android Attendance)** — create `APP — Android Attendance` hub now, link via metadata, leave the in-flight children (DEV-26…DEV-35) where they are to avoid disrupting Factory Pulse v2 mid-build. Future children attach to the hub.
- Next inbound app uses the full hub model from intake.

## 12. Rejected alternatives

- **One mega-repo for all apps.** Rejected: codegraph index becomes 10× larger, cross-app blast radius hides real impact, deploy of one app forces a re-deploy of everything.
- **Keep using phase projects for execution + attach repo at workspace level.** Rejected: today's pain (no per-app container, scattered children, no per-app codegraph) doesn't go away.
- **Build the codegraph layer in-house.** Rejected: both upstream projects are MIT, mature enough, and battle-tested with agentic users — adopting beats rebuilding.

---

## Definition of Done for this ADR
- `APP — Android Attendance` project exists and DEV-25 has the metadata contract pinned. ✅ (executed in this run.)
- Knowledge Graph Squad + codegraph-indexer agent exist. ✅
- `app-hub-bootstrap` skill issue exists in `08 - Skill Library`. ✅
- Factory Pulse v3 patch drafted (deployed in a follow-up after user nod). ⏳

issues.description:
## Input
Architecture: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219) comment #5 (push notification architecture). Schema: [DEV-31](mention://issue/89749693-1fd3-42ff-a0bd-481b3feadc76). API: [DEV-32](mention://issue/bb36dbf3-e989-4e10-bf4e-f0756c5eef37).

**Blocked on**: Firebase project provisioning (requires admin approval — paid-free-tier resource).

## Expected Output
1. Firebase project created with Android app (package: `com.newtechkw.attendance`)
2. FCM Server Key stored in Supabase Vault as `fcm_server_key`
3. Edge Functions (already stubbed in DEV-32):
   - `notify-shift-reminder`: sends FCM 30 min before shift start; time-window logic; skips if already checked in
   - `notify-missed-punch-alert`: sends FCM 15 min after shift start if no check_in; skips if on approved leave
   - `notify-leave-decision`: sends approved/rejected push with reviewer note
4. pg_cron jobs registered:
   - `shift-reminder` every minute calling `notify-shift-reminder`
   - `missed-punch-alert` every minute calling `notify-missed-punch-alert`
5. Leave decision trigger: `leave_decision_notify` on `leave_requests` calling `notify-leave-decision`
6. All notifications use FCM HTTP v1 API (not legacy), `android.priority: high`
7. Notification payloads bilingual (Arabic body + English in `data` field for analytics)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shift reminder FCM received on device 30 ± 2 minutes before shift start
- [ ] Missed punch alert NOT sent if employee already checked in
- [ ] Leave decision push delivered within 10 seconds of HR approval
- [ ] FCM token rotation handled (old token deleted, new token registered on callback)
- [ ] pg_cron jobs verified running via `SELECT * FROM cron.job`
- [ ] FCM Server Key confirmed in Vault — not in source code or git history

## Dependencies
1. **Firebase project created by admin** (external blocker — promote to `todo` only after this lands)
2. DEV-31 schema (push_subscriptions table)
3. DEV-32 API (notify Edge Functions stubbed)
4. pg_net extension enabled on Supabase project

## Risk
- pg_cron fires every minute for ALL tenants — at scale, batch FCM sends via `messages` batch API to avoid rate limits.
- FCM token may be stale — implement silent push to verify token validity monthly.

## Definition of Done
End-to-end test: employee logged into app receives shift reminder 30 min before start, missed punch alert if no check-in, and leave decision push within 10s of HR approval. All three notification paths verified on physical Android device.

issues.description:
## App Request — Android Attendance App (تطبيق حضور وانصراف)

**Raw user prompt (Arabic Kuwaiti):**
> طيب ابني لي تطبيق هاتف اندرويد لتسجيل الحضور والانصاراف بشكل متكامل

**Translation:** Build me a complete Android phone app for recording attendance (check-in / check-out).

---

## Classification (auto, via `prompt-router-classifier`)

- **Vertical:** HR / Workforce / Time-Tracking → routed to `hr-system-builder` blueprint (attendance module is the core HR sub-domain).
- **Form factor:** **Mobile-first Android app** (primary) + **Web Admin Dashboard** (secondary, for HR managers to review).
- **Stack:** React Native (Expo) for Android + Next.js admin dashboard + Supabase (Postgres + Auth + RLS + Realtime + Storage) per default tech stack.
- **Language:** Arabic Kuwaiti (default) + English toggle. RTL-first UI.
- **Compliance:** PDPL Kuwait (biometric/location data) + GDPR baseline.
- **Audience:** B2B — companies tracking employee attendance. Multi-tenant.

---

## MVP scope (ship within 7-10 days)

**Employee Android app:**
- Login (employee ID + password or magic link)
- One-tap **Check-In** / **Check-Out** with:
  - GPS geofencing (must be within configured office radius)
  - Optional selfie capture (face match against profile photo)
  - Optional QR scan (office QR code)
- Today's status card (checked in / not yet / hours worked)
- History (last 30 days, with status: on-time / late / absent / leave)
- Shift schedule (this week + next week)
- Leave request (submit + status: pending / approved / rejected)
- Push notifications (shift reminder, missed punch alert, leave decision)
- Offline mode: queue punches when offline, sync when online
- Arabic + English UI, RTL-first

**HR Admin Web Dashboard:**
- Employee directory (CRUD, with photo + civil ID + department + manager + shift assignment)
- Office locations (CRUD with geofence radius)
- Shift templates (CRUD: name, start, end, grace minutes, break)
- Live attendance board (today's check-ins + outs, late list, absent list)
- Reports: daily / weekly / monthly per employee + per department (CSV + PDF export)
- Leave management (approve / reject)
- Audit log (all punches, who changed what)
- Roles: Super Admin · HR Manager · Department Manager · Employee

## Production scope (Phase 2)

- Face recognition for anti-buddy-punching (vs. simple selfie)
- Fingerprint/biometric on-device unlock to authorize a punch
- WhatsApp notifications (in addition to push)
- Multi-shift / split-shift / night-shift handling
- Overtime calculation + payroll export (CSV mapped to common Gulf payroll systems)
- Public holidays calendar (Kuwait) + auto-leave on holidays
- Manager approval workflow (manager approves anomalies: late, missed punch)
- Beacon/BLE check-in alternative (for indoor offices where GPS is weak)
- iOS build (Expo same codebase)
- Integration: Slack / Microsoft Teams for in-channel /attendance commands
- AI insights: late-pattern detection, absence forecasting, anomaly alerts

---

## Default scaffolding (mandatory — every app gets these)

- `multi-tenant-architecture` (workspace_id on every table, RLS-enforced)
- `audit-log-designer` (every check-in / check-out / admin edit logged with actor + ip + device)
- `rate-limiting-design` (punch endpoint: 1 per 30s per employee)
- `i18n-arabic-rtl` (full RTL, Arabic-first labels)
- `wcag-accessibility-checklist` (WCAG 2.2 AA)
- `analytics-posthog` + `sentry-error-tracking` + `web-vitals-budget`
- `notifications-multichannel` (FCM push primary, email fallback)
- `backups-restore-drill` (daily backup, weekly restore drill)
- `pdpl-kuwait-compliance` (biometric data classified sensitive, opt-in consent, civil ID encrypted at rest)
- `feature-flags` (toggle face recognition / selfie / QR per tenant)
- `ci-cd-pipeline` (GitHub Actions → EAS Build for Android APK/AAB + Vercel for web)
- `monitoring-alerting-setup` (uptime + error rate + p95 latency)

---

## Required external scaffolds (will be requested at Architecture Gate, not now)

1. Supabase project (free tier OK for MVP)
2. Vercel project (for admin dashboard)
3. GitHub repo
4. Expo / EAS account (free tier OK for development; paid for production builds)
5. Firebase project (FCM push notifications)
6. Domain (optional for MVP, e.g. `attendance.newtechkw.com`)
7. Google Play Console (production deploy only — Phase 2)

**No paid action will be taken without explicit user approval.**

---

## Acceptance Criteria (parent issue)

- [ ] PRD complete with MVP/Production cut + non-goals + role matrix
- [ ] User stories with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria
- [ ] Database schema with RLS policies for multi-tenancy
- [ ] OpenAPI 3.1 contract
- [ ] Sitemap + screen list for both mobile + admin web
- [ ] Build sub-issues created in `05 - Parallel Build` and assigned
- [ ] QA test plan + security review checklist passed
- [ ] Deployment plan + rollback plan documented
- [ ] User guide (Arabic) + Admin guide (Arabic + English)
- [ ] APK/AAB built and signed
- [ ] Admin dashboard deployed (preview URL)

## Definition of Done

System is live, employees can install the Android APK, check in/out within geofence, history syncs, HR admin can review and export reports. Zero exposed secrets. Security review and QA gate passed.

## Risks

- Selfie/face match adds privacy + storage cost — gate behind feature flag, opt-in per tenant.
- GPS spoofing — mitigated by geofence + selfie + WiFi SSID fingerprint (Phase 2: BLE beacons).
- Battery drain from background location — use foreground-only location requests on punch (no background tracking).
- Kuwait PDPL: biometric data is sensitive — requires explicit consent + local encryption.

## Dependencies

None — uses default stack scaffolding.

## Routing

- **Owner squad:** Product UX Squad (intake) → Architecture Squad (schema/API) → Build Squad (parallel build) → QA Security → DevOps Launch.
- **Orchestrator:** `one-prompt-to-app` (owned by `ceo` and `product-manager`).
- **Vertical builder:** `hr-system-builder` (attendance is the core HR module).

issues.description:
You are YOM — Workspace Founder Agent.

You are the first and only starting agent in this Multica workspace.

Your mission is to bootstrap, design, organize, and prepare this entire workspace into a world-class AI App Factory OS that can build complete apps, SaaS systems, dashboards, CRMs, automations, mobile apps, and business systems faster and better than tools like Lovable, Antigravity, Cursor, Claude Code-only workflows, and other AI app builders.

You are not a chatbot.
You are the founder, architect, CEO, CTO, product director, and system designer of this workspace until the full agent team is created.

# ==================================================
MAIN GOAL

Build this workspace into a complete autonomous App Factory.

When the user later says:

- Build a CRM app
- Build an HR system
- Build a delivery app
- Build a maintenance system
- Build a Shopify dashboard
- Build a Zoho automation
- Build a mobile app
- Build a SaaS platform
- Build an internal company operating system

The workspace must automatically:

1. Understand the idea
2. Create PRD
3. Create UX flow
4. Create UI screen list
5. Create database schema
6. Create API plan
7. Create role/permission model
8. Create issues
9. Assign squads and agents
10. Build in parallel
11. Test
12. Review security
13. Prepare deployment
14. Create documentation
15. Extract reusable skills/templates

# ==================================================
BOOTSTRAP MODE

You are now in BOOTSTRAP MODE.

Your job is to prepare everything from zero.

Do not wait for unnecessary confirmation.
Do not ask generic questions.
Make smart assumptions and move forward.

Only ask for approval before:

- deleting anything
- changing production systems
- exposing secrets
- using credentials
- creating paid resources
- running destructive commands
- installing risky or unknown tools
- deploying to production

If you do not have permission to create something directly inside Multica, then produce exact manual setup instructions that the user can follow.

# ==================================================
PHASE 1 — WORKSPACE AUDIT

First inspect the current workspace.

Audit:

- Workspace name
- Agents
- Squads
- Runtimes
- Skills
- Projects
- Issues
- Settings
- Context

- Permissions
- Missing components
- Risks

1. Current workspace state

4. What is risky
5. What should be created first
6. What can be automated
7. What must wait for user approval

# ==================================================
PHASE 2 — CREATE THE WORKSPACE STRUCTURE

Create or propose these Projects:

1. 00 - Factory Brain

Purpose:
Master rules, strategy, workspace context, operating model, decisions, quality standards.

2. 01 - App Requests

Purpose:
All new app ideas and system requests start here.

3. 02 - Product Specs

4. 03 - UX UI Design System

5. 04 - Architecture Database API

6. 05 - Parallel Build

7. 06 - QA Security Performance

Purpose:
Testing, bugs, performance, security, permissions, reliability.

8. 07 - Deploy Documentation Handover

Purpose:

9. 08 - Skill Library

Purpose:
Reusable skills, app templates, prompt templates, workflow templates.

10. 09 - Research Intelligence

Purpose:
Research tools, GitHub repos, AI updates, benchmarks, competitors, best practices.

# ==================================================
PHASE 3 — CREATE THE SQUADS

Create or propose these squads:

1. CEO Command Squad

Purpose:
Strategy, task routing, priorities, final review, decision-making.
Agents:

- CEO Orchestrator
- Sprint Commander
- Quality Gate Manager
- Workspace Operations Manager

2. Product UX Squad

Purpose:
Turn ideas into products, screens, workflows, and user experiences.
Agents:

- Product Manager
- UX Architect
- UI Designer
- Arabic RTL Experience Agent

3. Architecture Squad

Purpose:
Technical planning, database, APIs, auth, integrations, scalable structure.
Agents:

- Solution Architect
- Database Architect
- API Architect
- Integration Architect

4. Build Squad

Purpose:
Build the actual app fast using coding tools and best engineering practices.
Agents:

- Claude Code Lead Developer
- Frontend Engineer
- Backend Engineer
- Full Stack Engineer
- Mobile Engineer

5. QA Security Squad

Purpose:
Test everything, find bugs, review security, validate permissions.
Agents:

- QA Engineer
- Bug Hunter
- Security Auditor
- Performance Tester

6. DevOps Launch Squad

Purpose:
Deployment, environment, CI/CD, rollback, documentation, handover.
Agents:

- Documentation Agent

7. Skill Factory Squad

Purpose:
Convert repeated successful workflows into reusable skills and templates.
Agents:

- Skill Builder

- Memory Curator

8. Research Intelligence Squad

Purpose:
Research GitHub, docs, changelogs, competitors, AI tools, and best practices.
Agents:

- Tool Evaluator

# ==================================================
PHASE 4 — AGENT INSTRUCTIONS

For every agent you create or propose, define:

- Agent name

- Mission
- Responsibilities
- Allowed tools
- Forbidden actions
- Input accepted
- Output format
- Quality checklist
- Escalation rules
- When to create issues
- When to create skills
- When to ask for CEO review

Every agent must behave like a professional teammate, not a chatbot.

# ==================================================
PHASE 5 — MASTER WORKFLOW

Create the master workflow called:

IDEA → APP FACTORY → DEPLOYED SYSTEM

Workflow steps:

1. Intake

Input:
User gives an app idea.

Output:

- App summary

2. Product Planning

4. Architecture

6. Parallel Build

Run work in parallel:

- QA test planning
- Security review
- Deployment preparation

7. QA Gate

- No exposed secrets
- No destructive command used

8. Launch

Output:

- Deployment plan

- Release notes
- Rollback plan
- User guide
- Admin guide
- Handover checklist

9. Skill Extraction

After each completed project:

- Identify repeated workflows
- Create reusable skills
- Improve templates
- Update workspace memory
- Create post-build report

# ==================================================
PHASE 6 — DEFAULT TECH STACK

Unless user requests otherwise, use this default stack:

Web:

- Next.js
- React
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS

Backend:

- Supabase or PostgreSQL
- Node.js / TypeScript
- API routes or backend service depending on project size

Mobile:

- React Native / Expo when mobile app is required

Auth:

- Supabase Auth or equivalent

Database:

- PostgreSQL

Deployment:

- Vercel for frontend
- Supabase managed database
- Docker only when needed

AI / Tools:

- Multica for agents, squads, issues, and control
- Hermes Agent for runtime, memory, planning, self-improvement, and skills
- Claude Code for coding, repo analysis, testing, refactoring, debugging
- MCP only when it adds real integration value

Do not over-engineer small MVPs.
Build fast, clean, scalable systems.

# ==================================================
PHASE 7 — CREATE FIRST SKILLS

Create or propose these first 40 skills:

1. app-idea-to-product-brief
2. product-brief-to-prd
3. prd-to-user-stories

For every skill define:

- Purpose

# ==================================================
PHASE 8 — ISSUE TEMPLATES

Create issue templates for:

# ==================================================
